text.title: Backlog Grooming and Roadmapping Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Backlog Grooming and Roadmapping. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Grooming runs on a fixed weekly cadence; missed weeks are logged as risk.
rule.rationale: Skipping grooming for 2+ weeks lets stale items dominate the top of the list.

rule.statement: Every backlog item carries a size estimate AND a priority score after each grooming pass; un-scored items are top of the next pass.

rationale.source: Mike Cohn — Agile Estimating and Planning

rule.rationale: Un-scored items hide cost; sized items can be traded against capacity.

rule.statement: Every item is tagged with the active quarter theme OR explicitly marked 'theme-orphan' for review.
rule.rationale: Theme-orphans accumulate; tagging makes them visible for explicit decisions.

rule.statement: Roadmap is split into three horizons (1 month / 3 month / 12 month); items move only one band per grooming.

rationale.source: Geoffrey Moore — three horizons of growth

rule.rationale: Multi-band jumps signal panic re-planning; one band per week keeps drift visible.

rule.statement: Items with no movement for 90 days are culled or promoted; the backlog never grows unboundedly.
rule.rationale: An unbounded backlog cannot be groomed thoroughly; staleness pressure forces decisions.

rule.statement: Each horizon band has a named owner accountable for its health.
rule.rationale: Anonymous bands rot; named owners feel the pressure to keep them clean.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-backlog-grooming-roadmapping.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
